growth_records.medium: Fumarate medium (freshwater medium, acetate donor / fumarate acceptor)

up.method: exponential-phase analyte/gDW slope x growth rate; HPLC (Aminex 87-H); triplicate; ±0.10

sec.method: 1:1 dcuB fumarate->succinate reduction; individual succinate production rate measured by HPLC but numeric value not tabulated in main text

growth_records.cite: Adam M Feist et al. Constraint-Based Modeling of Carbon Fixation and the Energetics of Electron Transfer in Geobacter metallireducens. PLoS Computational Biology (2014). DOI:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1003575. [PMC3998878]
growth_records.snippet: GS-15 dcuB Fumarate/Batch: Growth Rate 0.114±0.005 hr-1; Donor Uptake Rate 6.81±0.10; Acceptor Uptake Rate 14.0±0.2; Acceptor/Donor Ratio 2.06±0.01 (Table 3).
growth_records.notes: Table 3, experimental (phenomic) row, GS-15 dcuB, Fumarate/Batch, donor acetate. Measured mu=0.114 hr-1 => doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.114 = 6.08 h. Uptake/production rates computed by authors as (analyte mmol per gDW from least-squares fit over exponential phase) x growth rate; averaged over 3 biological replicates. Fumarate uptake calculated from net fumarate+malate. Growth rate similar to previously reported 0.105 hr-1 (Butler et al). Donor concentration 15 mM acetate per Methods. Full fumarate/freshwater medium composition cited from Lovley ref [39] / Butler ref [40], not reproduced in text -> medium_composition_stated:false.

growth_records.cite: Adam M Feist et al. Constraint-Based Modeling of Carbon Fixation and the Energetics of Electron Transfer in Geobacter metallireducens. PLoS Computational Biology (2014). DOI:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1003575. [PMC3998878]
growth_records.snippet: GS-15 dcuB Fumarate/Chemostat (Donor-limited): Growth Rate 0.05; Donor Uptake Rate 2.79±0.23; Acceptor Uptake Rate N.D. (Table 3).
growth_records.notes: Table 3, GS-15 dcuB, Fumarate/Chemostat, acetate-limited. At steady state growth_rate = set dilution rate D = 0.05 hr-1 (mu=D). doubling_time = ln2/0.05 = 13.86 h. Measured acetate uptake 2.79 mmol/gDW/h. dcuB chemostat medium: 5 mM acetate + 27.5 mM fumarate (Methods). Acceptor uptake Not Determined.

up.method: exponential-phase analyte/gDW x growth rate; HPLC; ±2.08

growth_records.cite: Adam M Feist et al. Constraint-Based Modeling of Carbon Fixation and the Energetics of Electron Transfer in Geobacter metallireducens. PLoS Computational Biology (2014). DOI:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1003575. [PMC3998878]
growth_records.snippet: GS-15 Nitrate/Batch (byproduct ammonia): Growth Rate 0.111±0.022; Donor Uptake 5.20±2.08; Acceptor Uptake 4.67±0.64 (no3) -3.98±0.38 (nh4); Acceptor/Donor 0.90±0.42 (Table 3).
growth_records.notes: Table 3, wild-type GS-15, Nitrate/Batch, donor acetate. Measured mu=0.111 hr-1 => doubling_time=ln2/0.111=6.24 h. Acetate uptake 5.20, nitrate uptake 4.67, ammonia production 3.98 mmol/gDW/h (nh4 listed as negative -3.98 in table indicating production). Rates = (mmol/gDW from exponential-phase least-squares) x growth rate, 3 replicates. Error on acceptor rates uses 90% CI from Lovley & Phillips 1988 [27]. Donor 15 mM acetate.

growth_records.medium: Ferric citrate medium, acetate-limited continuous culture

up.method: electron acceptor (Fe(III)->Fe(II)) reduction rate; ferrozine assay; ±9.08

sec.method: stoichiometric with Fe(III) reduced (ferrozine assay); equals acceptor uptake

growth_records.cite: Adam M Feist et al. Constraint-Based Modeling of Carbon Fixation and the Energetics of Electron Transfer in Geobacter metallireducens. PLoS Computational Biology (2014). DOI:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1003575. [PMC3998878]
growth_records.snippet: GS-15 Fe(III)/Chemostat (Donor-limited): Growth Rate 0.05; Donor Uptake 7.86±1.02; Acceptor Uptake 58.29±9.08; Acceptor/Donor Ratio 7.41±0.64 (Table 3).
growth_records.notes: Table 3, wild-type GS-15, Fe(III)/Chemostat, acetate(donor)-limited. Steady-state growth_rate = D = 0.05 hr-1 (mu=D); doubling_time=ln2/0.05=13.86 h. Measured acetate uptake 7.86, Fe(III) reduction 58.29 mmol/gDW/h. Fe(II) production equals Fe(III) reduced (ferrozine assay). WT chemostat medium: 5 mM acetate + 55 mM ferric citrate (Methods). Rates protein-normalized via protein=0.4344*DCW.

up.units: rate not quantified numerically in text

sec.units: rate not quantified numerically in text (acid added; net proton CONSUMPTION during fumarate reduction)
sec.method: pH-stat: amount of acid added to hold pH ~7

growth_records.cite: Srinivasan, Karthikeyan et al. Characterization of proton production and consumption associated with microbial metabolism. BMC Biotechnology (2010). DOI:10.1186/1472-6750-10-2. [PMC2831035]
growth_records.method: reported (OD600 monitored; 1.0 OD600 = 0.47 gDW/L); no explicit mu given
growth_records.snippet: batch culture of G. metallireducens (c); acid added (d) ... Geobacter strains showed a similar trend in the amount of acid added as compared to the aerobic growth of E. coli with acetate ... indicating proton consumption
growth_records.notes: G. metallireducens anaerobic, acetate(10 mM)/fumarate(40 mM), 30 C, N2-sparged, pH ~7 (freshwater fumarate medium, Esteve-Nunez et al., referenced). Measured signal = acid added -> net proton CONSUMPTION during fumarate reduction, same qualitative trend as G. sulfurreducens on fumarate. No numeric growth or uptake rate in text; strain designation not given.

derived.qs_mu_note: pooled across studies/media — not a single chemostat series, so no maintenance model fitted
